creator: david@catwhisker.org
text:
As shown in the typescript in <http://www.catwhisker.org/~david/FreeBSD/ports/>, 'pkg upgrade' can encounter an error that prevents it from actually upgrading anything, but then terminate with an exit status of 0.

Salient excerpts from <http://www.catwhisker.org/~david/FreeBSD/ports/typescript_pkg_upgrade>:

Script started on Sun Oct 28 05:33:41 2018
...

	gnome-online-accounts-3.28.0
	libgdata-0.17.9

pkg: Fail to create temporary file: /usr/local/libexec/gimp/2.2/plug-ins/align-layers/.align-layers.pD2NdeK0jYaA:Not a directory

[17/47] Extracting gimp-app-2.10.6_1,1: 100%

Command exit status: 0
Script done on Sun Oct 28 05:34:25 2018

The rest of the typescript from the above documents my (eventually successful) attempts to circumvent the problem.

This was on a system running:
FreeBSD albert.catwhisker.org 11.2-STABLE FreeBSD 11.2-STABLE #797  r339829M/339839: Sun Oct 28 03:39:42 PDT 2018     root@freebeast.catwhisker.org:/common/S1/obj/usr/src/sys/ALBERT  amd64


I note that this is the first time (of which I am aware) that I have encountered this issue since starting to use a weekly 'pkg upgrade' on this system since July, 2015.
